name: Login Endpoint Rate Limit
objective: Limit POST requests to the login endpoint to a maximum of 5 requests per IP per minute, blocking credential stuffing and brute-force login attacks
risk_mitigated: Brute-force password attacks, credential stuffing, account enumeration via login endpoint
potential_side_effects: Legitimate users on shared IP addresses (e.g., corporate NAT, large office networks) may hit the rate limit if many users attempt to log in simultaneously. Consider using a higher threshold or a 'challenge' action instead of 'block' for shared IP scenarios.
tuning_notes: Adjust the threshold (currently 5 req/min) based on your user base. For high-traffic apps, 10-20 req/min per IP is still effective against brute force while reducing friction for shared IPs. Consider adding a CAPTCHA challenge action before switching to a hard block.
deployment_notes: Start with a 'log' action to observe your baseline login traffic. Identify legitimate high-frequency login patterns (batch SSO callbacks, automated testing) before switching to 'block'. Coordinate with application teams.
monitoring_notes: Monitor rate limit events. A sustained high rate of limit hits from a single ASN or subnet may indicate an ongoing credential stuffing attack. Consider automatically blocking the source ASN via a separate firewall rule.

cloudflare_rate_limit_config.description: Login brute-force rate limit — k1N pack v1.0

response.body: {"error": "Too many login attempts. Please wait before trying again."}
